heading: Development and conservation of reliable sources
label: 98h–6 Development and conservation of reliable sources

children.text: by purchasing, or making a commitment to purchase, strategic and critical materials from reliable sources when such materials are needed for the stockpile;

by contracting with facilities located in and owned and controlled by reliable sources, or making a commitment to contract with such facilities, for the processing or refining of strategic and critical materials in the stockpile when processing or refining is necessary to convert such materials into a form more suitable for storage or disposition or meeting stockpile requirements;
by qualifying facilities located in and owned and controlled by reliable sources, or qualifying strategic and critical materials produced by such facilities, to meet stockpile requirements;

by contracting with facilities located in and owned and controlled by reliable sources to recycle strategic and critical materials to meet stockpile requirements or increase the balance of the National Defense Stockpile Transaction Fund under ; andsection 98h of this title

children.label: A
children.text: limits the liability of the stockpile to not more than the total funding provided by the Federal Government;

children.label: B
children.text: limits the funding contribution of the Federal Government to not more than 50 percent of the cost of the bankable feasibility study; and

children.label: C
children.text: does not obligate the Federal Government to purchase strategic and critical materials from the reliable source.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term of a contract or commitment made under subsection (a) may not exceed ten years.

children.heading: Preexisting contracts
children.label: B Preexisting contracts
children.text: A contract entered into before , for a term of more than ten years may be extended, on or after , for a total of not more than an additional ten years pursuant to any option or options set forth in the contract.December 22, 2023December 22, 2023

children.heading: Matters relating to co-funding of bankable feasibility studies
children.label: 2 Matters relating to co-funding of bankable feasibility studies
children.text: To the extent authorized by Congress pursuant to the Defense Production Act of 1950 ( et seq.) and determined to be required by the President pursuant to that Act, the National Defense Stockpile Manager may provide for loans or procure debt issued by other entities to carry out a project for the development of strategic and critical materials with respect to which a study was carried out under subsection (a)(5).50 U.S.C. 4501

children.heading: Proposed transactions included in annual materials plan
children.label: c Proposed transactions included in annual materials plan
children.text: Descriptions of proposed transactions under subsection (a) shall be included in the Annual Materials and Operations Plan. Changes to any such transaction, or the addition of a transaction not included in such plan, shall be made in accordance with .section 98d(a)(2) of this title

children.heading: Availability of funds
children.label: d Availability of funds
children.text: The authority of the National Defense Stockpile Manager to enter into obligations under this section is effective for any fiscal year only to the extent that funds in the National Defense Stockpile Transaction Fund under are adequate to meet such obligations.section 98h of this title

of the selected option for a strategic and critical materials development project that includes appropriately detailed assessments of realistically assumed extraction, processing, metallurgical, economic, marketing, legal, environmental, social, and governmental considerations and any other relevant operational factors and detailed financial analysis, that are necessary to demonstrate at the time of reporting that production is reasonably justified; and
that may reasonably serve as the basis for a final decision by a proponent of a project or financial institution to proceed with, or finance, the development of the project.
